card.intuition: Observability is the ability to understand what is happening inside your system by looking at the external data it produces: Logs (Events), Metrics (Aggregates), and Traces (Journeys).
card.analogy: A hospital patient: **Metrics** are their heart rate and temperature (numbers over time). **Logs** are the doctor's notes ('Patient complained of headache at 2 PM'). **Traces** are the record of the patient moving from Triage to X-Ray to Surgery.
when_to_use: Setting up alerts for when error rates (Metrics) spike
Following a single user's request through 10 different microservices (Traces)
gotchas: Monitoring is 'Is it working?'. Observability is 'Why is it not working?'.
Data overload: if you log every single variable, your logging bill will eventually be higher than your server bill. Use 'Sampling' and 'Log Levels'.

exercise.prompt: Identify the three pillars of observability.
exercise.starter_code:
pillars = [
    "1. _____: Textual records of events",
    "2. _______: Numerical data points over time",
    "3. ______: End-to-end request journeys"
]
exercise.test_code:
assert "logs" in solution.lower()
assert "metrics" in solution.lower()
print("PASS")
